title: Inject ConfigMap as Environment Variables
description:
Deploy an nginx Pod that reads database connection settings from a ConfigMap as environment variables using `envFrom`. This pattern keeps your container image generic — the same image runs in dev and prod by swapping the ConfigMap.

**Common Pitfalls:** ConfigMap must exist before the Pod starts, or the pod will fail with `CreateContainerConfigError`. Env vars from ConfigMaps are NOT updated in running pods — restart the pod to pick up ConfigMap changes.
steps: Define a ConfigMap named 'db-config' with keys DB_HOST and APP_ENV
Define a Pod named 'webapp' using image nginx:alpine
Add envFrom.configMapRef in the container spec pointing to 'db-config'
Apply the manifest with kubectl apply -f
Verify env vars with: kubectl exec webapp -- env | grep -E 'DB_HOST|APP_ENV'
hints: ConfigMap and Pod can be in the same YAML file separated by ---
envFrom is a list under the container spec, not under spec directly
configMapRef takes a name field matching your ConfigMap's metadata.name
kubectl exec <pod> -- env lists all environment variables in the container
Set DB_HOST to 'postgres.default.svc' and APP_ENV to 'production' in the ConfigMap data

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: db-config
data:
  DB_HOST: # TODO: use 'postgres.default.svc'
  APP_ENV: # TODO: use 'production'
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: webapp
spec:
  containers:
  - name: webapp
    image: nginx:alpine
    envFrom: # TODO: Add configMapRef pointing to 'db-config'

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: db-config
data:
  DB_HOST: "postgres.default.svc"
  APP_ENV: "production"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: webapp
spec:
  containers:
  - name: webapp
    image: nginx:alpine
    envFrom:
    - configMapRef:
        name: db-config
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo 'Checking ConfigMap db-config exists...'
if ! kubectl get configmap db-config &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ ConfigMap db-config not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ ConfigMap db-config exists'

echo 'Checking DB_HOST value...'
DB_HOST=$(kubectl get configmap db-config -o jsonpath='{.data.DB_HOST}')
if [ "$DB_HOST" != "postgres.default.svc" ]; then
  echo "✗ DB_HOST expected 'postgres.default.svc', got '$DB_HOST'"
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ DB_HOST is correct'

echo 'Checking APP_ENV value...'
APP_ENV=$(kubectl get configmap db-config -o jsonpath='{.data.APP_ENV}')
if [ "$APP_ENV" != "production" ]; then
  echo "✗ APP_ENV expected 'production', got '$APP_ENV'"
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ APP_ENV is correct'

echo 'Waiting for Pod webapp to be Running...'
kubectl wait pod webapp --for=condition=Ready --timeout=60s
echo '✓ Pod webapp is Running'

echo 'Verifying env vars inside pod...'
ENV_CHECK=$(kubectl exec webapp -- env 2>/dev/null)
if ! echo "$ENV_CHECK" | grep -q 'DB_HOST=postgres.default.svc'; then
  echo '✗ DB_HOST not found in pod environment'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ DB_HOST visible inside pod'

if ! echo "$ENV_CHECK" | grep -q 'APP_ENV=production'; then
  echo '✗ APP_ENV not found in pod environment'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ APP_ENV visible inside pod'

echo ''
echo 'All checks passed!'
exit 0
